channel.title: SOC Intelligence Digest
channel.description: Seven-day operational threat intelligence and IOC reporting for SOC review. Coverage window: 2026/07/17 12:44 - 2026/07/24 12:44 JST. Article dates: 2026/07/17 - 2026/07/24 JST.

item.title: Chaos ransomware deploys browser-based msaRAT to evade network detection
item.description: Source: Security Affairs / Source attributes: Italy / News / Secondary / Categories: Security, Malware, Threat Intel, Ransomware, Network, Detection / Topics: Cisco, Ransomware, Malware / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (76) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 3 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, JA attack activity, campaign priority, coherent attack event, detection evidence context, indicator context, reported by 3 sources, primary references 1 / Summary: Cisco Talos uncovered msaRAT, a Chaos ransomware RAT that hides C2 traffic by routing it through Chrome or Edge using the Chrome DevTools Protocol. Cisco Talos disclosed msaRAT, a Rust-based remote access trojan attributed to the Chaos ransomware group that routes its entire command-and-control channel through the victim’s own Chrome or Edge browser. The malware […] / Also reported by: Help Net Security, Bleeping Computer / Primary source: Talos https://blog.talosintelligence.com/chaos-msarat-living-off-the-browser-to-build-covert-c2-channel/

item.title: What Happened Between OpenAI and Hugging Face?
item.description: Source: Rapid7 / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Incident Response, Threat Intel, Research, Exploit, Zero-Day, Supply Chain, Identity, AI Security, Endpoint, Network, Detection / Topics: Microsoft, Supply Chain, Exploit, Zero-Day, Identity, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, primary references 1 / Summary: The OpenAI and Hugging Face incident lands like a warning shot for anyone thinking seriously about frontier AI and cybersecurity research. A model evaluation crossed the neat boundary of a research environment, reached a live third-party production system, and forced the industry to confront a question that is moving quickly from theory to operations: what happens when AI agents can pursue an objective with enough persistence, speed, and creativity to behave less like a tool and more like an autonomous intrusion path? According to OpenAI’s disclosure, the incident began during an internal eval / Primary source: Rapid7 https://www.rapid7.com/blog/post/ai-openai-hugging-face-what-happened / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: CVE-2026-16232: Critical Check Point SmartConsole Authentication Bypass Exploited in the Wild
item.description: Source: Rapid7 / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Research, Vulnerability, Exploit, Patch, Cloud, Network, Application Security / Topics: CVE-2026-16232, Network Edge, Vulnerability, Exploit, Cloud / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: active exploitation (rank 4) / Reference count: 4 / Reasons: focus: active exploitation, EN attack activity, EN IOC/detection/hunting, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, active exploitation priority, IOC 9, linked IOC evidence, target and impact context, vulnerability exploitation context, indicator context, reported by 4 sources, primary references 1 / Summary: Overview On July 22, 2026, Check Point published a security advisory for multiple vulnerabilities affecting Security Management, Multi-Domain Management, and firewall products. The most urgent of these is CVE-2026-16232 , an authentication bypass in the SmartConsole login process classified as improper authentication ( CWE-287 ). CVE-2026-16232 has been assigned a critical CVSS score of 9.1. The vulnerability allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to obtain an application login token and authenticate to the management server with full administrative privileges, enabling modification of secu / Also reported by: Help Net Security, The HackersNews, Security Affairs / Primary source: Rapid7 https://www.rapid7.com/blog/post/etr-cve-2026-16232-critical-check-point-smartconsole-authentication-bypass-exploited-in-the-wild / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Months-long breach exposes South Korean diplomats’ personal data
item.description: Source: Help Net Security / Source attributes: Croatia / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Data Breach, Incident Response / Topics: Data Breach / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (83) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN Japan/Asia relevance, EN critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, Japan related, critical sector, primary references 2 / Summary: South Korea’s Foreign Ministry has disclosed that attackers breached the Korea National Diplomatic Academy’s online education system, compromising personal data belonging to current and former ministry staff and diplomats stationed abroad. The Korea National Diplomatic Academy launched the online training platform in 2022 to support remote learning during the COVID-19 pandemic. Since then, it has been used to deliver job training and language courses for diplomatic personnel. According to the ministry, the intrusion started in … More → The post Months-long breach exposes South Korean diplomats / Primary source: mofa.go.kr https://www.mofa.go.kr/www/brd/m_4075/view.do?seq=369430

item.title: Chaos ransomware's msaRAT: Living off the browser to build a covert C2 channel
item.description: Source: Talos / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Malware, Ransomware / Topics: Ransomware, Malware / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, JA attack activity, campaign priority, coherent attack event, indicator context, primary references 1 / Summary: The Chaos ransomware group uses new malware "msaRAT" that hijacks browsers. The malware doesn't communicate directly with C2 but connects through the browser. It enables arbitrary command execution while hiding the attacker's IP from victims via WebRTC over TURN. / Primary source: Talos https://blog.talosintelligence.com/chaos-msarat-living-off-the-browser-to-build-covert-c2-channel/ / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Hackers Lurked for 10 Months Inside South Korea Diplomatic System
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Vulnerability, Threat Actor, Data Breach, Mobile, Network / Topics: Network Edge, Vulnerability, Data Breach / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (87) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 2 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN Japan/Asia relevance, EN critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, compound vulnerability exploit impact detection context, Japan related, critical sector, reported by 2 sources, primary references 1 / Summary: The National Diplomatic Academy data breach has raised significant cybersecurity concerns in South Korea after the Ministry of Foreign Affairs confirmed that hackers maintained access to the academy's online education system for nearly 10 months. The cyberattack resulted in the exposure of personal information belonging to current and former ministry employees, including diplomats serving overseas. According to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the attackers exploited a vulnerability in the National Diplomatic Academy's online education platform in April 2025. The compromise remained active unt / Also reported by: Bleeping Computer / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/knowledge-hub/what-is-a-data-breach/

item.title: OpenAI and Hugging Face Investigate AI Models’ Cyber Breakout
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Vulnerability, Exploit, Zero-Day, Patch, Supply Chain, Data Breach, AI Security, Network, Incident Response / Topics: Network Edge, Supply Chain, Vulnerability, Exploit, Zero-Day, Data Breach, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (83) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, indicator context, primary references 2 / Summary: OpenAI and Hugging Face are investigating an AI security incident involving an AI agent that compromised infrastructure while models were being evaluated for advanced cyber capabilities. The incident was detected and contained after the models identified and chained vulnerabilities across OpenAI’s research environment and Hugging Face’s production infrastructure. Hugging Face disclosed the incident last week, while OpenAI has now shared preliminary findings from its investigation. According to OpenAI, the activity involved a combination of models, including GPT 5.6 Sol and a more capable pre-r / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/exploit/

The Russian state-supported advanced persistent threat (APT) group’s activity is tracked in the cybersecurity community under several names (see Cybersecurity industry tracking ), primarily as “LAUNDRY BEAR,” a name initially coined by the Netherlands Gen / Primary source: CISA https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa26-204a / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Qilin Ransomware Attackers Exploit PAN-OS Authentication Bypass for Initial Access

Arctic Wolf Labs said it investigated multiple intrusions in June 2026 that began with the exploitation of CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), an authentication bypass flaw affecting the portal and gateway / Primary source: ArcticWolf https://arcticwolf.com/resources/blog/exploitation-of-cve-2026-0257-leads-to-qilin-ransomware/

item.title: Your AI agent’s config is now the payload: How attackers are targeting the developer agent harness
item.description: Source: Tenable / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Exposure Management, Threat Intel, Malware, Supply Chain, Identity, AI Security / Topics: Microsoft, Supply Chain, Malware, Identity, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, indicator context, primary references 1 / Summary: Attackers have shifted from hiding from AI tools to running inside them. By poisoning the config files that govern AI coding assistants, a new worm class achieves silent persistence, evades AI-based scanners, and spreads across an organization's repositories through developers' own tools. Key takeaways AI coding assistant configuration files, such as settings.json hooks, .cursorrules Cursor MDC rules, and similar harness files, are now explicit targets in supply-chain attacks, not collateral damage. These files simultaneously sit at the intersection of three trust relationships: The developer / Primary source: Tenable https://www.tenable.com/blog/ai-coding-assistant-agent-harness-attacks / Primary source age: 0 days

Brand impersonation remains one of the most common tactics used to establish trust with victims. / Primary source: Cofense https://cofense.com/blog/click-to-sync-from-google-ads-maintenance-notice-to-credential-theft / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Targeted Attack on Government Entities in the Middle East | Part 1
item.description: Source: Zscaler / Source attributes: United States / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, APT, Threat Actor, Endpoint, Network, Application Security / Topics: Microsoft, Malware, APT / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: APT / targeted campaign (rank 6) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: APT / targeted campaign, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, EN targeted/APT/state-backed, EN Japan/Asia relevance, EN critical sector, JA attack activity, APT/campaign priority, IOC 12, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, Japan related, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: IntroductionIn July 2026, Zscaler ThreatLabz observed new activity by a threat actor with links to East Asia targeting government entities in the Middle East. During analysis, ThreatLabz captured post-compromise activity and uncovered previously undocumented malware tooling, including TELESHIM, MIXEDKEY, and BINDCLOAK. The campaign used a multi-stage attack chain to establish and maintain access on infected systems, with TELESHIM abusing the Telegram API for command-and-control (C2) communication to blend in with legitimate internet traffic.This blog post (Part 1) explores the technical detail / Primary source: Zscaler https://www.zscaler.com/blogs/security-research/targeted-attack-government-entities-middle-east-part-1 / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: APTグループ「Sednit」が活動を再開ークラウドを活用した高度な攻撃手法が判明
item.description: Source: Canon MJ ESET Security / Source attributes: Japan / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Malware, Exploit, Phishing, APT, Threat Actor, Endpoint, Incident Response, Application Security / Topics: APT28, Linux, Malware, Exploit, Phishing, APT / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: APT / targeted campaign (rank 6) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: APT / targeted campaign, EN targeted/APT/state-backed, EN Japan/Asia relevance, JA attack activity, JA detection/analysis, JA targeted/APT/state-backed, JA critical sector, APT/campaign priority, IOC 3, linked IOC evidence, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, Japan related, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: ESET社は、2つのインプラント「BeardShell」と「Covenant」を中心とした「デュアル（二重）インプラント」手法を利用するSednitの高度なインプラントチームが活動を再開したことを発見しました。 本記事では、その手法の特徴と攻撃の実態について解説します。 本記事はESET Japanが提供する「ESETブログ」に掲載された「Sednit再始動：戦線に復帰」を再編集したものです。 2024年4月以降、Sednitの高度な技術力を持つ開発チームが再始動しました。このチームは、レジリエンスを高める目的で異なるクラウドプロバイダーを利用する2つのインプラント「BeardShell」と「Covenant」を中心とした、最新のツールキットを携えています。この「デュアル（二重）インプラント」手法により、ウクライナ軍関係者に対する長期的な監視が可能となりました。興味深いことに、これら最新のツールセットのコードには、2010年代に同グループが使用していたインプラントの直接的な系譜が見て取れます。 本ブログの要点： ESET社の研究チームは、キーロガー「SlimAgent」がウクライナで展開された2024年の事例をきっかけに、Sednitの高度なインプラントチームが活動を再開したことを突き止めました SlimAgentのコードは、2010年代のSednitの主要なバックドアであったXa / Primary source: Canon MJ ESET Security https://eset-info.canon-its.jp/malware_info/special/detail/260721.html / Primary source age: 0 days

Group-IB, which named the malware HollowGraph, says the approach moves tasking and stolen data through legitimate Microsoft Graph API traffic, so the activity looks / Primary source: Group-IB https://www.group-ib.com/blog/hollowgraph-microsoft-365/

item.title: From a Single Alert to 1,000 Files: Inside an Exposed WebDAV Malware Delivery Lab
item.description: Source: Rapid7 / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Malware, Phishing, Threat Intel, Research, Vulnerability, Data Breach, AI Security, Endpoint, Network, Detection / Topics: Microsoft, Malware, Phishing, Vulnerability, Data Breach, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, campaign priority, IOC 13, coherent attack event, target and impact context, detection evidence context, indicator context, primary references 1 / Summary: Executive summary An MDR alert recently led our team to an exposed server that was doing more than hosting payloads. It was functioning as a fully operational malware delivery lab. Containing over 1,000 artifacts, the infrastructure served as a QA hub where attackers systematically tested delivery paths, social engineering lures, and WebDAV execution methods. Our analysis reveals an interesting shift in adversary operations: attackers are adopting generative AI to move beyond individual exploits and operate like modern software product teams. By leveraging LLMs for rapid lure generation, detai / Primary source: Rapid7 https://www.rapid7.com/blog/post/tr-exposed-webdav-malware-delivery-lab-analysis / Primary source age: 0 days

WordPress is one of the most widely deployed content management systems, making vulnerabilities in its core software potentially significant for organizations operating public-facing websites. item.title: CVE-2026-58644: Microsoft SharePoint Server Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
item.description: Source: Rapid7 / Source attributes: United States / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Research, Vulnerability, Exploit, Patch, Network, Incident Response, Application Security / Topics: CVE-2026-58644, Microsoft, Web Application, Vulnerability, Exploit / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 100 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: active exploitation (rank 4) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: active exploitation, EN attack activity, EN IOC/detection/hunting, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, active exploitation priority, IOC 1, linked IOC evidence, target and impact context, detection evidence context, vulnerability exploitation context, compound vulnerability exploit impact detection context, indicator context, primary references 1 / Summary: Overview On July 14, 2026, Microsoft published a security advisory addressing CVE-2026-58644 , a critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability affecting on-premises Microsoft SharePoint Server deployments. The vulnerability, which carries a CVSS v3.1 score of 9.8 (Critical), results from the deserialization of untrusted data ( CWE-502 ) and allows an unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code. Microsoft confirmed active exploitation of CVE-2026-58644, and the vulnerability was subsequently added to CISA’s Known Exploited Vulnerabilities ( KEV ) catalog on July 16, 2026. In paralle / Primary source: Rapid7 https://www.rapid7.com/blog/post/etr-cve-2026-58644-microsoft-sharepoint-server-unauthenticated-remote-code-execution-vulnerability-exploited-in-the-wild / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: Oracle July 2026 Patch Fixes 1,434 CVEs Across 334 Products
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intel, Exploit, Zero-Day, Patch, Threat Actor, Supply Chain, Cloud, AI Security, Network / Topics: Network Edge, Supply Chain, Vulnerability, Exploit, Zero-Day, Cloud, AI Security / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 99 / Confidence: High (77) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN active exploit/PoC/zero-day, EN detection/analysis, EN critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, critical sector, primary references 1 / Summary: Oracle has released its July 2026 Critical Patch Update, delivering one of its largest quarterly security releases to date. The latest Oracle security patch addresses more than 1,400 vulnerabilities across hundreds of products, with the company indicating that artificial intelligence likely played a significant role in identifying most of the flaws. According to Oracle, the July 2026 Critical Patch Update contains 1,449 security patches, covering 1,434 unique Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) across 334 products. July 2026 Critical Patch Update Covers Hundreds of Oracle Products The / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/exploit/

item.title: Hackers Deface Kenya President William Ruto’s Website, Demand $330K Ransom
item.description: Source: The Cyber Express / Source attributes: Global / News / Secondary / Categories: Threat Intel, Data Breach, Network, Incident Response, Cryptocurrency / Topics: Network Edge, Data Breach / Priority: Critical (100) / SOC: 98 / Confidence: High (73) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, EN critical sector, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, uncertain reporting, critical sector, primary references 2 / Summary: Kenya is investigating a Kenya cyberattack that temporarily defaced President William Ruto’s official website with an anti-government message and a Bitcoin ransom demand for five bitcoins, reportedly worth about $330,000. The attackers replaced the website’s homepage with the message, displayed a cryptocurrency wallet address and threatened to publish unspecified information about President William Ruto unless the ransom was paid. The website was hacked on Saturday, July 18, 2026. Following the incident, access to the presidential website was temporarily restricted as authorities began contain / Primary source: Cyble https://cyble.com/knowledge-hub/what-is-a-cyber-attack/

item.title: 白い南京錠に閉ざされたファイル、WhiteLock ランサムウェアの警告
item.description: Source: AhnLab / Source attributes: South Korea / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Malware, Ransomware, Endpoint / Topics: Microsoft, Ransomware, Malware / Priority: Critical (90) / SOC: 76 / Confidence: High (97) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, JA attack activity, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, uncertain reporting, primary references 1 / Summary: ファイルが次々と暗号化され、さらにリモートアクセスツールまで停止した場合、すでにランサムウェア感染が進行している可能性がある。最近確認された WhiteLock ランサムウェアは、Windows 環境の主要なファイルを暗号化した後、ランサムノートを生成して身代金を要求する。暗号化の過程で外部サーバーと通信し、AnyDesk や TeamViewer などのリモートアクセスツール関連サービスを停止させることで被害者のリモート対応を妨害する点が特徴である。 本ブログでは、WhiteLock ランサムウェアの主な動作とランサムウェア対策において確認すべきセキュリティポイントについて解説する。 WhiteLock は、感染したシステム上のファイルを暗号化し、その復号と引き換えに身代金を要求する典型的なランサムウェアである。暗号化されたファイルには「.fbin」拡張子が追加され、感染システムには「c0ntact.txt」というランサムノートが生成される。 WhiteLock... / Primary source: AhnLab https://asec.ahnlab.com/jp/94554/ / Primary source age: 0 days

item.description: Source: IoT OT Security News / Source attributes: Japan / News / Secondary / Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intel, Exploit, Threat Actor, Cloud, ICS/OT, Endpoint / Topics: CVE-2026-53565, Microsoft, Operational Technology, Vulnerability, Exploit, Cloud, ICS/OT / Priority: Critical (83) / SOC: 60 / Confidence: High (83) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, EN detection/analysis, campaign priority, coherent attack event, target and impact context, Japan source, primary references 2 / Summary: Citrix Secure Access and Endpoint Client for Windows Vulnerability Enables Privilege Escalation2026/07/18 CyberSecurityNews — Cloud Software Group が公表したのは、Citrix Secure Access Client for Windows／Endpoint Analysis Client for Windows に影響を及ぼす 2 件のセキュリティ脆弱性に関する情報である。そのうち 1 件の脆弱性を悪用する攻撃者は、影響を受けるマシン上で SYSTEM アクセスを取得できる。この深刻な脆弱性 CVE-2026-53565 (CVSS v4.0 8.5) は、不適切な権限管理 (CWE-269) に起因する。ローカル・アクセスを持つ標準の低権限のユーザーであっても、Windows システム上で最高権限レベルである SYSTEM レベルへと権限を昇格できる。 この脆弱性は、Citrix の Secure Access Client for Windows／Endpoint Analysis Client for Windows … Continue reading "Citrix の脆弱性 CVE-2026-53565／53566 が F / Primary source: cybersecuritynews.com https://cybersecuritynews.com/citrix-secure-access-and-endpoint-vulnerability/

item.title: RondoDox to begin DDoS attacks
item.description: Source: NICTER Blog / Source attributes: Japan / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Detection, Exploit, Malware, DDoS, Cryptocurrency / Topics: Exploit, Malware / Priority: Critical (82) / SOC: 92 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: IOC / detection evidence (rank 2) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: IOC / detection evidence, EN attack activity, EN detection/analysis, JA attack activity, IOC 15, linked IOC evidence, indicator context, Japan source, primary references 1, IOC/detection-only priority cap / Summary: On January 13 2026, we have only reported that the RondoDox is mining Monero 1 though it has the ability of DDoS attack like the other IoT botnets. The reason we didn’t report about the DDoS attack functionality was that the malware itself was only keeping a connection with the command and control (C2) server by repeating keepalive commands except a few DDoS attack trials while the downloaded mining tool was running separately for a long time. About 10 months after the massive exploitation in July 2025, NICT Cyber Threat analysis Office has identidied that the RondoDox C2 began to send the DDo / Primary source: NICTER Blog https://blog.nicter.jp/2026/07/rondodox_2026_3g/ / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: カカオ (Kakao) アカウント移行に関するメールを受け取ったら？まず確認すべきポイント
item.description: Source: AhnLab / Source attributes: South Korea / Vendor / Primary / Categories: Phishing, Threat Actor / Topics: Phishing / Priority: Critical (82) / SOC: 64 / Confidence: High (100) / Focus: attack campaign / intrusion (rank 5) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: attack campaign / intrusion, JA attack activity, campaign priority, coherent attack event, uncertain reporting, primary references 1 / Summary: 最近、カカオ (Kakao) の公式案内メールを装った「アカウント移行案内」のフィッシングメールが確認された。このメールは、ユーザーのカカオアカウントが移行される予定であると装って不安をあおり、本文内の「アカウント利用確認」リンクをクリックするよう誘導する。ユーザーがリンク先のフィッシングページでメールアドレスとパスワードを入力すると、その認証情報が攻撃者の管理する外部サーバーへ送信される可能性がある。本ブログでは、カカオアカウント移行案内を装ったフィッシング手口と、ユーザーが注意すべきポイントについて紹介する。 ログインページのように見えても目的はアカウント情報の窃取 カカオアカウントは、メッセンジャー、ポータルサイト、各種オンラインサービスと連携しているため、攻撃者に頻繁に狙われるアカウントの1つである。ASEC は最近、北朝鮮関連の人物を標的としたカカオを装うフィッシングメールを確認した。 今回のフィッシングメールは、カカオアカウントの移行案内を装い、ユーザーの不安をあおるとともに、「アカウント利用確認」を名目として本文中のリンクをクリックさせる手口であった。「アカウント移行」「利用確認」「セキュリティ確認」といった、ユーザーに行動を急がせる表現は、フィッシングメールでよく使われるキーワードである。 [図1] カカオアカウント移行案内を装ったフィッシングメール本文 しかし、こ / Primary source: AhnLab https://asec.ahnlab.com/jp/94555/ / Primary source age: 0 days

item.title: RondoDoxがDDoS攻撃を開始
item.description: Source: NICTER Blog / Source attributes: Japan / Research / Primary / Categories: Threat Intel, Detection, Malware, DDoS, Cryptocurrency / Topics: Malware / Priority: Critical (81) / SOC: 93 / Confidence: High (93) / Focus: IOC / detection evidence (rank 2) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: IOC / detection evidence, EN attack activity, JA attack activity, JA detection/analysis, IOC 14, detection evidence context, indicator context, Japan source, primary references 1, IOC/detection-only priority cap / Summary: 2026年1月13日に RondoDox について報告しましたが 1 、実質的に暗号通貨 Monero のマイニングに特化していたため、当時は DDoS 機能については触れませんでした。他の IoT マルウェア同様に本体には DDoS 機能が実装されていますが、マイニングツールをダウンロードして起動した後は、DDoS 攻撃はほとんど観測されず、C2との接続を維持するために keepalive コマンドを定期的に繰り返すだけでした。しかし、5月になり、NICT サイバー脅威分析室は、RondoDox が DDoS 攻撃を開始したことを確認しました。2025年7月の大規模なエクスプロイトから約10ヶ月も経っていました。 / Primary source: NICTER Blog https://blog.nicter.jp/2026/07/rondodox_2026_3gj/ / Primary source age: 0 days

Employees at at least 29 organizations were compromised over two days in July, after searching for the Claude desktop app and clicking a sponsored Bing ad. The ad pointed to the genuine claude.ai domain, but landed on an attacker-published public artifact, which redirected them to a spoofed download site serving SectopRAT. What are Claude Artifacts? item.title: Chaos Ransomware Uses msaRAT to Route C2 Traffic Through Headless Chrome and Edge
item.description: Source: The HackersNews / Source attributes: United States / News / Secondary / Categories: Ransomware, Endpoint / Topics: Cisco, Microsoft, Ransomware / Priority: High (60) / SOC: 55 / Confidence: Medium (67) / Focus: background / low operational value (rank 0) / Reference count: 1 / Reasons: focus: background / low operational value, EN attack activity, JA attack activity, indicator context, primary references 3 / Summary: The Chaos ransomware group ran its command-and-control through the victim's own browser. Cisco Talos on Thursday detailed msaRAT, the Rust implant behind it, found on a compromised Windows machine ahead of the encryptor. The implant never opens an outbound connection of its own. Its process talks to 127.0.0.1 and nothing else. It starts Chrome or Edge in headless mode and drives the browser / Primary source: Talos https://blog.talosintelligence.com/chaos-msarat-living-off-the-browser-to-build-covert-c2-channel/
